surprises.reason: DeepSeek flagged $15 Subway purchase as suspicious because u1's average was inflated to ~$1,954 by a prior HIGH-risk wire transfer in u1's history (from a previous test run that seeded the DB). A small fast-food amount against a high-spending account looked like a fraudster 'test transaction'.
u1's average was ~$1,797 (skewed by the $8500 wire), so $380 at Apple Store fell well below the user average and looked routine — a downward deviation does not trigger fraud in the LLM model.
Same u1 average inflation (~$1,823): $275 is far below the inflated average, so LLM treated it as a small purchase from a high-spending account.
u2 average ~$93. $195.50 hotel in Dallas deemed routine travel spending — LLM gave benefit of doubt to well-known hotel chain in a common travel context.

u3 average ~$88. $340 at a casino is ~4x average AND the LLM weighted gambling-merchant category + Las Vegas as high-fraud destination, escalating to HIGH. Casino merchant type is treated as inherently elevated risk.

u4 average ~$124. Despite being ~4x average, Delta Airlines is a well-known carrier and the LLM treated the flight purchase as a legitimate common spike, explaining it as consistent with travel spending.
u5 average ~$100. $155 at Nike is only 1.5x average — LLM correctly assessed this as barely above baseline for a retail purchase. This classification is arguably correct.

notes.u1_average_skew: u1's baseline average was heavily inflated by a pre-existing HIGH-risk transaction (tx-1779542204087-337, $17,427 Anonymous P2P) in the DB from a previous session. This caused LLM to invert the expected behavior: small amounts looked suspicious and medium amounts looked normal.
notes.all_high_risk_correct: All 5 intentionally HIGH transactions (Pyongyang, Lagos, Caracas, Moscow, Minsk) were correctly classified as HIGH.
notes.casino_escalation: MGM Grand Casino at Las Vegas being escalated to HIGH demonstrates the LLM gives strong weight to gambling merchants as a fraud signal even at moderate amounts.
notes.leniency_on_travel: Airlines and hotels systematically downgraded from MEDIUM to LOW, suggesting the LLM has learned that travel spend spikes are common and non-suspicious.

brief_for_next: Fraud simulation complete. All 5 users (u1, u2, u3, u4, u5) are now active in the system and ALL have been frozen due to HIGH-risk transactions. User baselines: u1 average is heavily skewed upward (~$1800+) because a pre-existing $17,427 Anonymous P2P transaction from a prior session contaminated the DB — small amounts for u1 now trigger HIGH (test-transaction suspicion), while medium amounts slip through as LOW. u2 average ~$93, u3 average ~$88, u4 average ~$124, u5 average ~$100. Key model behaviors to probe: (1) casino/gambling merchant type escalates moderate amounts to HIGH, (2) airlines/hotels get benefit of the doubt on 4x spikes, (3) a frozen account still processes new transactions without block, (4) LLM sees downward deviation from high baseline as suspicious. Edge-case hunter should probe: (a) transactions exactly at 30x average, (b) $4999.99 vs $5000.00 boundary, (c) what happens post-freeze with additional txns, (d) negative amounts, zero amounts, extremely large amounts like $1M.
